query: What English test scores do I need to prove B2 proficiency for admission?

queries: What English test scores do I need to prove B2 proficiency for admission?

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
You must provide a proof of English language proficiency to be admitted to the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science or [English or multilingual master programmes](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) at the Universitiy of Vienna.

[## Recognised proofs at level B2](#c805007)

[## Recognised proofs at level C1](#c805011)

[## Recognised proofs at level C2](#c805018)

A proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for admission to the bachelor programme in [International Legal Studies](/en/entrance-exam/international-legal-studies/) and in [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/) as well as to multiple master programmes taught in English and multilingual master programmes.

- [Required English proficiency for admission to master programmes taught in foreign languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/)

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text: proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the bachelor programme in International Legal Studies or Mathematical Foundations of Data Science. How should I proceed? Upload the final transcript of your previous school year as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2" at "Further documents and proofs" in your application for admission in u:space. You have to upload the secondary-school leaving certificate with the exam in the subject English before you complete the admission procedure.

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement

  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 115 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 or higher
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 63, 64](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- Decree: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/02_pdf/20250205-13.pdf) (05.02.2025)
- For applications for admission until winter semester 2024/25 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2021_2022/2021_2022_30.pdf) (17.12.2021)
- For applications for admission for the summer semester 2025 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2024_2025/2024_2025_18.pdf) (23.10.2024)

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2
Proofs B2
retrieved.text:
- Secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English. The following conditions apply:
  - The secondary-school leaving certificate was completed at a school of an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
  - If the level of English proficiency is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded.
  - The Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate (Matura) of the Corona year 2021 is equal to other secondary-school leaving certificates.
- "IB diploma" according to the regulations of the "International Baccalaureate Organization" with the subject English.
- Certificate of the supplementary examination in the subject English from the University Preparation Programme of an Austrian University.
- "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" from an Austrian university with a successfully completed examination in the subject English.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement, which fulfills all of the following conditions:
  - full-time degree programme of at least two years and
  - taught entirely in English and
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

Any of the above mentioned proofs at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- English as first language ([see FAQ](#c805025)).
- Proof of erudite language: secondary-school leaving certificate, if the certificate was awarded at an institution
  - with English as the language of education
  - in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 110 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 (at least 7.5 per component)
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

A proof of English language proficiency at level C2 is required for the master programme in Translation, if one of the working languages is English.

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: Is proof of German language proficiency required?
retrieved.text:
All bachelor/diploma programmes as well as master programmes instructed in German require proof of [German language proficiency at level A2](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-a2/) at the time of application. Without such certification you cannot apply for admission.

To be admitted to the degree programme you need a proof of [German language proficiency at level C1](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-c1/). If you do not possess such a proof, the supplementary examination in German will be prescribed in your admission letter.

With this admission letter, you can be admitted as a non-degree student and attend a German course at the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) for up to four semesters.

Can it be accepted? No. Only the proofs and certificates mentioned above are accepted. - English is my first language, but I do not have any of the proofs mentioned above. Do I have to take an exam? If you do not have a proof of your language proficiency at level C1, the University of Vienna may prescribe an assessment by an institution chosen by the university in the course of the admission procedure. You have to pay any potentially occurring costs yourself. - I have a proof of English language proficiency at level C1. However, only a proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for my degree programme. Do I have to provide a separate proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the

retrieved.title: Proof of German language proficiency
heading_path: General information about the German language proficiency
retrieved.text: - You need a proof of German language proficiency at level A2 when applying for admission. - In order to acquire level C1, which is required for the admission to all bachelor/diploma programmes instructed in German and most master programmes, you may participate in a German course of the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) (VWU). - While attending the VWU, you have to [consider some things](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) regarding your admission to the University of Vienna. - In case you apply for a bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure, you will be able to participate in the procedure even though you do not yet have an adequate proof of German language proficiency. However, you must have this proof at the time of enrolment. - For the following master programmes, a proof of German language proficiency at level C1 or C2 is required for the application: - [Master programme in German as a Foreign and Second Language (C1)](/en/admission/master-programmes/german-as-a-foreign-and-second-language/) - [Master programme in Translation (C2)](https://transvienna.univie.ac.at/studium/masterstudium-translation/voraussetzungen/)It is not possible to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme in order to be admitted to these master programmes. - In case you apply for a [master programme instructed in English](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/#c157833) or the [bachelor programme Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/), you will not be able to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme, as no proof of German language proficiency is required.

retrieved.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure

You need German language proficiency for all bachelor's and diploma programmes. The only exception is Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (because it is instructed in English).

- When you apply, you must provide a proof of German language proficiency at level A2.
- You decide for yourself whether your level of German is sufficient for the written test. Dictionaries are not allowed during the test.
- To be admitted to the degree programme, you need level C1. To reach this level, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU).

Find out everything you need to know about the recognised German language certificates and the VWU on this page.

The further procedure depends which country you completed your previous studies in. - Ukraine Starting from the beginning of the application period for the summer semester 2027: If you have a Ukrainan secondary-school leaving certificate and are applying for admission to a bachelor’s or diploma programme, you will need the NMT (National Multi-Subject Test): - a minimum of 100 points required - validity: 4 years - applies to diplomas awarded starting in the 2021–2022 school year - must be completed in at least 4 subjects In addition, your Ukrainian educational documents require an [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828). - United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland ("A-levels") If you receive the General Certificate of Education (GCE/"A-Levels"), you have to fulfil the following requirements: - Four general subjects that are independent from one another have to be completed with grade A\*, A, B or C (respectively grades 9 - 4). These subjects must include: - a foreign language - Mathematics - one of the subjects Biology, Physics or Chemistry - a subject of the Humanities - At least three of the four subjects have to be completed at the GCE Advanced level, the

retrieved.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level A2
retrieved.text:
- #### Proofs

  We only accept these proofs. There are no exceptions!

  - School report of a secondary-school including the subject German
  - Passed courses of 8 ECTS credits or 200 hours instructed in German within a degree programme at a recognised post-secondary educational institution

  The proofs mentioned here are valid indefinitely.
- #### Certificates

  We only accept these certificates. There are no exceptions!

  - Österreichisches Sprachdiplom – A2 (ÖSD Zertifikat A2, ZA2) or higher
  - Goethe Institut – Goethe Zertifikat A2 or higher
  - telc Deutsch – A2 or higher
  - Deutsches Sprachdiplom der Kultusministerkonferenz Stufe I – DSD I or higher
  - Österreichischer Integrationsfonds (ÖIF) – ÖIF Test (A2) or higher
  - Language Center of an Austrian University – successfully passed exam of level A2 or higher
  - Test Deutsch als Fremdsprache (Test DaF) with at least level TDN 3 in all parts or higher

  The certificates may not be older than 2 years.

retrieved.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
FAQ Knowledge of German
retrieved.text: Collapse all Open all #### I have a German language certificate at a higher level than A2. Do I need to have an A2 certificate as well? No, but: If you already have a German language certificate at level B1 or higher, please upload it with your application as a document type “Proof of German language proficiency at level A2”. This is a mandatory field. You cannot submit your application without uploading this document. #### I'll only receive my A2 certificate in a few weeks. Can I already submit my application? No, please only submit your application once you have all required documents. Applications without a proof of German language proficiency at level A2 will be rejected without exception. #### Do I have to take the German course at the VWU? Of course, you can first take a private German course, achieve the required German language proficiency and then complete the admission procedure at the University of Vienna. You can find the [recognised proofs of German at level C1](#c6332) further up on this page. You would like to take a German course in Austria, but not at the VWU? Please clarify with the Austrian Embassy, the Austrian Consulate or Magistratsabteilung 35 (Municipal Department 35) in advance what impact this will have on your residence permit. Once you have been admitted to the non-degree programme “University Programme Preparation Programme”, you must

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

- For applications for admission for the summer semester 2025 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2024_2025/2024_2025_18.pdf) (23.10.2024)

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2
Proofs B2
reranked.text:
- Secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English. The following conditions apply:
  - The secondary-school leaving certificate was completed at a school of an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
  - If the level of English proficiency is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded.
  - The Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate (Matura) of the Corona year 2021 is equal to other secondary-school leaving certificates.
- "IB diploma" according to the regulations of the "International Baccalaureate Organization" with the subject English.
- Certificate of the supplementary examination in the subject English from the University Preparation Programme of an Austrian University.
- "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" from an Austrian university with a successfully completed examination in the subject English.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement, which fulfills all of the following conditions:
  - full-time degree programme of at least two years and
  - taught entirely in English and
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

Any of the above mentioned proofs at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: FAQ
reranked.text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
reranked.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- English as first language ([see FAQ](#c805025)).
- Proof of erudite language: secondary-school leaving certificate, if the certificate was awarded at an institution
  - with English as the language of education
  - in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 110 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 (at least 7.5 per component)
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

A proof of English language proficiency at level C2 is required for the master programme in Translation, if one of the working languages is English.

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: Is proof of German language proficiency required?
reranked.text:
All bachelor/diploma programmes as well as master programmes instructed in German require proof of [German language proficiency at level A2](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-a2/) at the time of application. Without such certification you cannot apply for admission.

To be admitted to the degree programme you need a proof of [German language proficiency at level C1](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/german-language-proficiency-c1/). If you do not possess such a proof, the supplementary examination in German will be prescribed in your admission letter.

With this admission letter, you can be admitted as a non-degree student and attend a German course at the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) for up to four semesters.

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: FAQ
reranked.text: subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - My English certificate is not in the list. Can it be accepted? No. Only the proofs and certificates mentioned above are accepted. - English is my first language, but I do not have any of the proofs mentioned above. Do I have to take an exam? If you do not have a proof of your language proficiency at level C1, the University of Vienna may prescribe an assessment by an institution chosen by the university in the course of the admission procedure. You have to pay any potentially occurring costs yourself. - I have a proof of English language proficiency at level C1. However, only a proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for my degree programme. Do I have to provide a separate proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the

reranked.title: Proof of German language proficiency
heading_path: General information about the German language proficiency
reranked.text: - You need a proof of German language proficiency at level A2 when applying for admission. - In order to acquire level C1, which is required for the admission to all bachelor/diploma programmes instructed in German and most master programmes, you may participate in a German course of the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna) (VWU). - While attending the VWU, you have to [consider some things](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) regarding your admission to the University of Vienna. - In case you apply for a bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure, you will be able to participate in the procedure even though you do not yet have an adequate proof of German language proficiency. However, you must have this proof at the time of enrolment. - For the following master programmes, a proof of German language proficiency at level C1 or C2 is required for the application: - [Master programme in German as a Foreign and Second Language (C1)](/en/admission/master-programmes/german-as-a-foreign-and-second-language/) - [Master programme in Translation (C2)](https://transvienna.univie.ac.at/studium/masterstudium-translation/voraussetzungen/)It is not possible to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme in order to be admitted to these master programmes. - In case you apply for a [master programme instructed in English](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/#c157833) or the [bachelor programme Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/), you will not be able to attend a German course in the University Preparation Programme, as no proof of German language proficiency is required.

reranked.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
reranked.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure

You need German language proficiency for all bachelor's and diploma programmes. The only exception is Mathematical Foundations of Data Science (because it is instructed in English).

- When you apply, you must provide a proof of German language proficiency at level A2.
- You decide for yourself whether your level of German is sufficient for the written test. Dictionaries are not allowed during the test.
- To be admitted to the degree programme, you need level C1. To reach this level, you can attend a German course as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU).

Find out everything you need to know about the recognised German language certificates and the VWU on this page.

reranked.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
German language proficiency at level A2
reranked.text:
- #### Proofs

  We only accept these proofs. There are no exceptions!

  - School report of a secondary-school including the subject German
  - Passed courses of 8 ECTS credits or 200 hours instructed in German within a degree programme at a recognised post-secondary educational institution

  The proofs mentioned here are valid indefinitely.
- #### Certificates

  We only accept these certificates. There are no exceptions!

  - Österreichisches Sprachdiplom – A2 (ÖSD Zertifikat A2, ZA2) or higher
  - Goethe Institut – Goethe Zertifikat A2 or higher
  - telc Deutsch – A2 or higher
  - Deutsches Sprachdiplom der Kultusministerkonferenz Stufe I – DSD I or higher
  - Österreichischer Integrationsfonds (ÖIF) – ÖIF Test (A2) or higher
  - Language Center of an Austrian University – successfully passed exam of level A2 or higher
  - Test Deutsch als Fremdsprache (Test DaF) with at least level TDN 3 in all parts or higher

  The certificates may not be older than 2 years.

reranked.title: German language proficiency for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
heading_path: We accept the following proofs of German
FAQ Knowledge of German
reranked.text: Collapse all Open all #### I have a German language certificate at a higher level than A2. Do I need to have an A2 certificate as well? No, but: If you already have a German language certificate at level B1 or higher, please upload it with your application as a document type “Proof of German language proficiency at level A2”. This is a mandatory field. You cannot submit your application without uploading this document. #### I'll only receive my A2 certificate in a few weeks. Can I already submit my application? No, please only submit your application once you have all required documents. Applications without a proof of German language proficiency at level A2 will be rejected without exception. #### Do I have to take the German course at the VWU? Of course, you can first take a private German course, achieve the required German language proficiency and then complete the admission procedure at the University of Vienna. You can find the [recognised proofs of German at level C1](#c6332) further up on this page. You would like to take a German course in Austria, but not at the VWU? Please clarify with the Austrian Embassy, the Austrian Consulate or Magistratsabteilung 35 (Municipal Department 35) in advance what impact this will have on your residence permit. Once you have been admitted to the non-degree programme “University Programme Preparation Programme”, you must

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

selected.title: Proof of English language proficiency
selected.text:
- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

selected.title: Proof of English language proficiency
selected.text:
You must provide a proof of English language proficiency to be admitted to the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science or [English or multilingual master programmes](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) at the Universitiy of Vienna.

[## Recognised proofs at level B2](#c805007)

[## Recognised proofs at level C1](#c805011)

[## Recognised proofs at level C2](#c805018)

A proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for admission to the bachelor programme in [International Legal Studies](/en/entrance-exam/international-legal-studies/) and in [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/) as well as to multiple master programmes taught in English and multilingual master programmes.

- [Required English proficiency for admission to master programmes taught in foreign languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/)

heading_path: FAQ
selected.text: proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the bachelor programme in International Legal Studies or Mathematical Foundations of Data Science. How should I proceed? Upload the final transcript of your previous school year as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2" at "Further documents and proofs" in your application for admission in u:space. You have to upload the secondary-school leaving certificate with the exam in the subject English before you complete the admission procedure.

  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

[2] Proof of English language proficiency
You must provide a proof of English language proficiency to be admitted to the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science or [English or multilingual master programmes](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) at the Universitiy of Vienna.

[## Recognised proofs at level B2](#c805007)

[## Recognised proofs at level C1](#c805011)

[## Recognised proofs at level C2](#c805018)

A proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for admission to the bachelor programme in [International Legal Studies](/en/entrance-exam/international-legal-studies/) and in [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/) as well as to multiple master programmes taught in English and multilingual master programmes.

- [Required English proficiency for admission to master programmes taught in foreign languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/)

[3] Proof of English language proficiency — FAQ
proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the bachelor programme in International Legal Studies or Mathematical Foundations of Data Science. How should I proceed? Upload the final transcript of your previous school year as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2" at "Further documents and proofs" in your application for admission in u:space. You have to upload the secondary-school leaving certificate with the exam in the subject English before you complete the admission procedure.

[4] Proof of English language proficiency
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement

  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 115 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 or higher
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 63, 64](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- Decree: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/02_pdf/20250205-13.pdf) (05.02.2025)
- For applications for admission until winter semester 2024/25 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2021_2022/2021_2022_30.pdf) (17.12.2021)
- For applications for admission for the summer semester 2025 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2024_2025/2024_2025_18.pdf) (23.10.2024)

[5] Proof of English language proficiency — Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2 > Proofs B2
- Secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English. The following conditions apply:
  - The secondary-school leaving certificate was completed at a school of an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
  - If the level of English proficiency is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded.
  - The Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate (Matura) of the Corona year 2021 is equal to other secondary-school leaving certificates.
- "IB diploma" according to the regulations of the "International Baccalaureate Organization" with the subject English.
- Certificate of the supplementary examination in the subject English from the University Preparation Programme of an Austrian University.
- "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" from an Austrian university with a successfully completed examination in the subject English.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement, which fulfills all of the following conditions:
  - full-time degree programme of at least two years and
  - taught entirely in English and
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

Any of the above mentioned proofs at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

[6] Proof of English language proficiency — FAQ
/ - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

Question: What English test scores do I need to prove B2 proficiency for admission?

generate.raw_response: To prove B2 proficiency, you need: TOEFL IBT 94 points or higher, IELTS Academic Overall Band Score 6.5 points, Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE) result level B2, or Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE) result level B2 [1].

answer: To prove B2 proficiency, you need: TOEFL IBT 94 points or higher, IELTS Academic Overall Band Score 6.5 points, Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE) result level B2, or Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE) result level B2 [1].
